reasoning: This is no longer a live forecast — the tournament has concluded, with Spain defeating Argentina 1-0 via a Ferran Torres goal in the second period of extra time to claim their second World Cup title. Spain finished as the tournament's most complete side, conceding just one goal across the whole event and closing on a record-tying 37-plus match unbeaten run built on Rodri's midfield control and a historically miserly defense. Argentina, the defending champions, reached the final on Messi-inspired late heroics but couldn't break down Spain's defense in the decider, taking runner-up. Morocco is listed as the retrospective dark horse, having again reached the last eight as the standout outsider of the field. With the result final, confidence is High because the outcome is settled fact rather than a projection.
